faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Common questions about Aether Identity and our enterprise solutions.

keycloak.title: How does Aether compare to Keycloak?
keycloak.answer: Aether offers a more modern architecture with better performance (<15ms token validation), easier setup, and enterprise support. Unlike Keycloak, Aether provides SLA guarantees and dedicated support.

migrate.title: Can I migrate from Auth0 or Okta?
migrate.answer: Yes, we provide migration tools and assistance to import your users, applications, and configurations from Auth0, Okta, or any other OAuth provider. Contact our team for a customized migration plan.

offline.title: What happens if I lose internet connectivity?
offline.answer: Aether supports air-gapped deployments and can operate fully offline. Your authentication services remain functional even without internet access.

free.title: Is there a free tier?
free.answer: Yes, Aether Community Edition is free and open source. For enterprise features, SLA guarantees, and support, contact our sales team.

updates.title: How often do you release updates?
updates.answer: We release monthly security updates and quarterly feature releases. All updates are thoroughly tested and documented.

hero.title: One Login for All Your Applications
hero.description: Provide a seamless, secure authentication experience across all your web and mobile applications. Universal Login unifies your brand while eliminating password fatigue.

capabilities.title: Everything You Need for Modern Authentication
capabilities.description: Built for developers who demand flexibility without compromising on security or user experience.
capabilities.universalAuthDesc: Seamless login experience across all your applications with a single set of credentials. Support for web, mobile, and desktop platforms.
capabilities.sessionSyncDesc: Real-time session state synchronization across all devices and applications. Users stay logged in seamlessly as they switch between apps.
capabilities.crossDeviceSsoDesc: Enterprise-grade single sign-on that works across web, iOS, Android, and native desktop applications with consistent UX.
capabilities.adaptiveAuthDesc: Risk-based authentication that adapts security requirements based on device, location, and user behavior patterns.
capabilities.customizableBrandingDesc: Fully white-label the login experience with custom themes, logos, and styling to maintain brand consistency.
capabilities.idpIntegrationDesc: Connect to existing identity providers including Okta, Auth0, Azure AD, or any SAML/OIDC compliant IdP.

code.title: Integrate in Minutes

features.passwordlessDesc: Eliminate passwords entirely with WebAuthn, magic links, or biometric authentication for frictionless user experience.
features.socialLoginDesc: Support for Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Apple, and 50+ other social identity providers out of the box.
features.enterpriseSsoDesc: SAML 2.0 and OIDC federation with Azure AD, Okta, OneLogin, and any SAML-compliant identity provider.
features.mfaDesc: TOTP, SMS, email, and WebAuthn second factors with flexible policies and remember device features.
features.progressiveProfilingDesc: Collect user attributes incrementally across sessions to reduce signup friction while building complete profiles.
features.accountRecoveryDesc: Secure account recovery flows with verified email, phone, or backup codes to prevent account lockouts.

deployment.cloudHostedDesc: Fully managed service with automatic scaling, global CDN, and enterprise SLAs. Deploy in minutes.
deployment.onPremiseDesc: Deploy within your infrastructure for complete data sovereignty and compliance requirements.
deployment.hybridDesc: Cloud-managed control plane with on-premise session storage for maximum flexibility.

compliance.title: Enterprise-Grade Security
compliance.description: Universal Login is built with security at its core, meeting the stringent requirements of regulated industries.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: Explore our documentation and guides to get started with Universal Login.
resources.caseStudyDesc: How Acme Corp reduced login friction by 70%

diff.title: How does Universal Login differ from traditional SSO?
diff.answer: Universal Login provides a modern, consistent authentication experience across all applications regardless of the underlying identity system. Unlike traditional SSO solutions, it offers a unified user experience with built-in session management, passwordless options, and adaptive security policies.

idp.title: Can I integrate with my existing identity provider?
idp.answer: Yes, Universal Login integrates with Okta, Azure AD, Auth0, Keycloak, and any SAML 2.0 or OIDC-compliant identity provider. You can federate authentication while maintaining your existing user directory.

customize.title: Is the login page customizable?
customize.answer: Absolutely. You can customize colors, logos, backgrounds, typography, and even the full HTML/CSS template. White-label options are available for enterprise deployments.

passwordless.title: How does passwordless authentication work?
passwordless.answer: Passwordless authentication uses WebAuthn (FIDO2) for biometric or hardware key authentication, magic links sent via email, or one-time codes via SMS. This eliminates password fatigue while improving security.

down.title: What happens if the identity provider goes down?
down.answer: Universal Login includes built-in failover mechanisms and session caching. Users with active sessions remain authenticated, and fallback authentication methods can be configured for emergencies.

cta.title: Simplify Your Authentication
cta.description: Give your users a seamless login experience across all applications. Try Universal Login today.

hero.title: Universal Single Sign-On for Your Infrastructure
hero.description: Connect all your applications through a unified authentication gateway. SAML, OAuth, and OpenID Connect with full protocol support and零 vendor lock-in.

capabilities.universalSsoDesc: Authenticate once, access everything. Seamless SSO across web, mobile, and desktop applications with zero friction.
capabilities.identityProtocolsDesc: Full support for SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, and OpenID Connect. Integrate with any identity provider or application.
capabilities.adaptiveMfaDesc: Risk-based MFA with TOTP, WebAuthn, SMS, email, and push notifications. Step-up authentication when needed.
capabilities.userManagementDesc: Unified user directory with group synchronization, attribute mapping, and role-based access control.
capabilities.appGatewayDesc: Reverse proxy with integrated authentication for legacy apps. Protect internal services without code changes.
capabilities.sessionFederationDesc: Shared session state across applications. One login propagates everywhere instantly.

cta.title: Ready to Modernize Your SSO?
cta.description: Deploy Aether SSO and gain complete control over your authentication infrastructure. Open source, self-hosted, enterprise-ready.

diff.title: How does Aether SSO differ from Auth0 or Okta?
diff.answer: Aether SSO is self-hosted, giving you complete control over your identity infrastructure. Unlike cloud services, there are no per-user fees, and your data never leaves your network. It's ideal for organizations with strict data sovereignty requirements.

multiIdp.title: Can Aether federate with multiple identity providers?
multiIdp.answer: Yes, Aether supports identity brokering - you can connect multiple IdPs (Okta, Azure AD, Ping Identity, etc.) and present a unified authentication interface to your applications.

legacy.title: Does Aether work with legacy applications?
legacy.answer: Yes, the built-in application gateway can protect any HTTP-based application without requiring code changes. It handles session management and can even bridge between SAML and OAuth.

migration.title: How do I migrate from an existing SSO solution?
migration.answer: We provide migration tooling for Okta, Auth0, and Azure AD. Our team can assist with metadata transfer, attribute mapping, and testing to ensure a smooth transition.

limits.title: Is there a limit on the number of applications?
limits.answer: No, Aether has no application limits. You can register as many SP or IdP connections as needed. The limiting factor is your infrastructure capacity.

included.title: What is included in the Enterprise plan?
included.answer: The Enterprise plan includes unlimited users, all MFA methods, 24/7 dedicated support, SLA guarantee, custom integrations, security reviews, a dedicated account manager, and air-gapped deployment options.

contract.title: Can we get a custom contract?
contract.answer: Yes, we offer flexible contract terms including annual and multi-year options. Contact our sales team to discuss your specific requirements.

onpremise.title: Do you offer on-premise deployment?
onpremise.answer: Yes, we support fully on-premise, cloud, and hybrid deployments. Our enterprise plan includes air-gapped deployment for organizations with strict security requirements.

support.title: What kind of support do you provide?
support.answer: Enterprise customers receive 24/7 priority support with dedicated account managers, guaranteed response times, and access to our security team for architecture reviews.

migration.title: Can we migrate from our current provider?

hero.title: Complete Identity Lifecycle Management
hero.description: Centralize user provisioning, deprovisioning, and profile management across your entire organization. Scale to millions of users with enterprise-grade performance and security.

capabilities.title: Enterprise-Grade User Management
capabilities.description: Built for organizations that need complete control over their user identity lifecycle with advanced automation, compliance, and integration capabilities.
capabilities.lifecycleDesc: Complete user onboarding, profile management, and offboarding workflows with automated role assignment and deprovisioning.
capabilities.searchDesc: Powerful search with filters, pagination, and real-time results across millions of user records with sub-millisecond performance.
capabilities.rbacDesc: Granular permission management with hierarchical roles, custom attributes, and dynamic access policies based on user context.
capabilities.monitoringDesc: Live dashboard of user activities, session tracking, and behavioral analytics to detect anomalies and ensure compliance.
capabilities.auditDesc: Complete history of all user actions, modifications, and system events with tamper-proof logging and export capabilities.
capabilities.automationDesc: Automated user provisioning, approval workflows, and scheduled tasks to reduce manual overhead and ensure consistency.

features.title: Everything You Need to Manage Users
features.description: Comprehensive tools to handle user identity at any scale, from small teams to global enterprises.
features.bulkDesc: Import, update, or delete thousands of users in a single operation with CSV or JSON upload.
features.attributesDesc: Extend user profiles with custom fields, schemas, and validation rules specific to your organization.
features.delegationDesc: Grant limited admin access to department managers without full system privileges.
features.passwordDesc: Configurable password complexity, expiration, history, and multi-factor authentication requirements.
features.lifecycleDesc: Automated triggers for onboarding, transfers, suspensions, and termination based on HR events.
features.reportingDesc: Built-in reports on user activity, compliance status, and administrative actions with export options.

code.title: Programmatic User Management
code.description: Our REST API and SDKs give you full control over user lifecycle operations. Automate provisioning, build custom admin portals, or integrate with your existing tools.

integrations.title: Seamless Integrations

compliance.title: Built for Regulated Industries

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Common questions about Aether User Management.

directory.title: How does user management integrate with our existing directory?
directory.answer: Aether User Management supports bidirectional synchronization with Active Directory, LDAP, and other identity sources. Changes made in either system are automatically synced, ensuring consistent user data across your infrastructure.

bulk.title: Can we bulk import users from our HR system?
bulk.answer: Yes, our bulk import feature supports CSV and JSON formats with field mapping, validation rules, and error reporting. You can also set up automated imports from Workday, SAP, or any system via our API.

deprovisioning.title: How do you handle user deprovisioning?
deprovisioning.answer: When a user is terminated, Aether automatically revokes all access, removes from groups, archives their data, and can trigger offboarding workflows in connected systems like email, cloud storage, and SaaS applications.

limits.title: Is there a limit on the number of users we can manage?
limits.answer: Aether User Management is designed to scale to millions of users. Our distributed architecture ensures consistent performance regardless of user count, with horizontal scaling capabilities for enterprise deployments.

custom.title: Can we customize the user data model?
custom.answer: Yes, you can define custom attributes, schemas, and validation rules that align with your organization's data requirements. Custom fields support various data types and can be used in workflows, reports, and access policies.

government.title: Government Agencies

partner.title: Partner Networks
partner.desc: Enable secure partner access to specific resources with organization-scoped permissions.

security.title: Enterprise-Grade Security
security.description: Organization-level security policies ensure complete isolation and compliance with industry regulations.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Common questions about organization management in Aether Identity.

multi.title: How does multi-organization support work?
multi.answer: Aether allows you to create multiple organizations under a single deployment. Each organization has its own users, applications, and security policies while sharing the same infrastructure.

partition.title: Can we partition users between organizations?
partition.answer: Yes, users can belong to multiple organizations with different roles and permissions in each. This enables cross-organization collaboration while maintaining isolation.

limit.title: Is there a limit on the number of organizations?
limit.answer: No hard limits. Aether supports unlimited organizations with efficient resource usage. Performance scales horizontally as needed.

branding.title: Can we customize branding per organization?
branding.answer: Yes, each organization can have custom logos, colors, and login page themes. This is ideal for SaaS providers offering white-label solutions.

permissions.title: How do cross-organization permissions work?
permissions.answer: You can define permissions that apply across organizations or scoped to specific ones. This enables flexible collaboration while maintaining security boundaries.

cta.title: Scale Your Organization Management
cta.description: Get started with multi-organization support today. Contact our team for a customized demonstration.

hero.title: Enterprise MFA Without the Vendor Lock-In
hero.description: Secure authentication with TOTP, WebAuthn, SMS, email, and adaptive risk-based policies. Self-hosted, self-controlled, enterprise-grade.

capabilities.title: Complete Authentication Security
capabilities.description: Modern MFA solution with support for all authentication methods and adaptive security policies.
capabilities.webauthnDesc: Passwordless authentication using hardware keys, Touch ID, Face ID, and Windows Hello. Highest level of security with phishing-resistant credentials.
capabilities.totpDesc: Time-based one-time passwords compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator, and any RFC 6238 compliant app.
capabilities.emailDesc: Secure email-based verification codes with templated messages, branding options, and delivery tracking.
capabilities.smsDesc: Two-factor authentication via SMS with support for multiple providers, fallback options, and rate limiting.
capabilities.adaptiveDesc: Machine learning-powered risk analysis that automatically steps up authentication for suspicious logins.
capabilities.hardwareDesc: Support for YubiKey, Titan Security Key, and other FIDO2-compatible tokens. Enterprise-grade key management and enrollment.

features.title: Advanced MFA Features
features.description: Enterprise features for maximum security without sacrificing user experience.
features.passwordlessDesc: Eliminate passwords entirely with WebAuthn and magic links
features.stepUpDesc: Increase trust level for sensitive actions
features.enrollmentDesc: Guided user enrollment with backup methods
features.recoveryDesc: Secure account recovery without compromising security
features.policyDesc: Granular controls over when and how MFA is required
features.complianceDesc: Complete audit trail for regulatory requirements

comparison.title: How We Compare
comparison.description: See why organizations choose Aether MFA over cloud-only alternatives.

code.title: Easy Integration
code.description: Simple SDKs make adding MFA to your applications straightforward. Support for all major authentication methods.

benefits.title: Why Choose Aether MFA?
benefits.description: The most secure and flexible MFA solution for modern enterprises.
benefits.vendorLockinDesc: Self-hosted solution means you own your authentication infrastructure. Migrate anytime with standard protocols.
benefits.frictionlessDesc: Adaptive authentication means trusted users get seamless access while suspicious activity triggers additional verification.
benefits.phishingDesc: WebAuthn credentials are bound to specific origins, making them impossible to phish. Hardware-backed security for your users.
benefits.complianceDesc: Full audit logging, SOC 2 compliance, GDPR ready. Meet the strictest regulatory requirements with detailed authentication logs.

duo.title: How does Aether MFA compare to Duo Security?
duo.answer: Aether MFA offers all the features of Duo with self-hosted deployment, no per-user pricing, and full data sovereignty. You retain complete control over your authentication infrastructure.

idp.title: Can Aether integrate with our existing IdP?
idp.answer: Yes, Aether MFA integrates with any OAuth 2.0/OIDC provider, SAML IdP, or can operate as a standalone MFA solution. It works alongside Okta, Azure AD, Keycloak, and more.

loss.title: What happens if a user loses their second factor?
loss.answer: Aether provides multiple recovery options including backup codes, trusted device recovery, and admin-initiated recovery with identity verification. Recovery is logged for compliance.

passwordless.title: Is passwordless authentication supported?
passwordless.answer: Yes, Aether fully supports WebAuthn/FIDO2 for passwordless authentication. Users can authenticate with hardware keys, biometric sensors, or device-based credentials.

enforce.title: Can we enforce MFA for specific users or groups?
enforce.answer: Yes, Aether's policy engine allows granular enforcement rules based on user attributes, groups, IP ranges, risk scores, or any custom conditions.

cta.title: Secure Your Authentication Today
cta.description: Deploy Aether MFA and gain complete control over your authentication security. Open source, self-hosted, enterprise-ready.

hero.description: Implement RBAC, ABAC, or hybrid authorization policies with real-time evaluation, sub-millisecond latency, and complete audit trails.

features.title: Fine-Grained Authorization

features.rbacDesc: Define roles and hierarchies with inheritance, making it easy to manage permissions across large organizations.
features.abacDesc: Create dynamic policies based on user attributes, resource metadata, and contextual data like time, location, and device.
features.hierarchicalDesc: Support for nested groups, organizational units, and resource hierarchies with automatic permission propagation.
features.denyDesc: Implement explicit deny rules that take precedence, ensuring security-critical permissions can be locked down.
features.conditionsDesc: Express complex authorization logic with conditions, expressions, and custom functions that evaluate in real-time.
features.realtimeDesc: Sub-millisecond permission checks with cached policies and optimized evaluation engine for high-throughput APIs.

code.title: Authorization in Code
code.description: Define policies in code or JSON, test them locally, and deploy with confidence. Our SDKs make it easy to integrate authorization into your applications.

deployment.onPremiseDesc: Full control with air-gapped deployment support. Your authorization data never leaves your infrastructure.

industries.title: Built for Regulated Industries

industries.healthcareDesc: HIPAA-compliant authorization for patient records, with role-based access to PHI and audit trails.
industries.financeDesc: SOX-compliant controls with segregation of duties, transaction limits, and regulatory reporting.
industries.governmentDesc: FedRAMP Ready solutions with classification-based access, need-to-know enforcement, and clearance levels.
industries.ecommerceDesc: Dynamic authorization based on user tier, order value, and fraud risk scores with real-time policy updates.

compliance.title: Enterprise-Grade Security
compliance.description: Aether FGA is designed to meet the stringent security and compliance requirements of healthcare, finance, government, and enterprise organizations.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Common questions about Aether FGA and authorization.

rbacVsAbac.title: What is the difference between RBAC and ABAC?
rbacVsAbac.answer: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) assigns permissions to roles which are then assigned to users. ABAC (Attribute-Based Access Control) uses attributes of the subject, resource, and environment to make authorization decisions. Aether supports both models and allows you to combine them for hybrid policies.

policyUpdates.title: How does Aether handle policy updates?
policyUpdates.answer: Aether uses a policy versioning system with atomic updates. Changes are validated before deployment, and you can roll back to previous versions. Real-time propagation to authorization nodes ensures sub-second policy updates across your infrastructure.

integration.title: Can Aether integrate with my existing identity provider?
integration.answer: Yes, Aether integrates with any OIDC or SAML identity provider. It can sync groups and attributes from Active Directory, LDAP, or cloud identity providers. The authorization engine uses these attributes for policy evaluation without duplicating user data.

freeTier.title: Is there a free tier for Aether FGA?
freeTier.answer: Yes, Aether FGA Community Edition is free and open source. For high-throughput requirements, enterprise SLAs, and dedicated support, contact our sales team.

performance.title: How do you ensure authorization performance?
performance.answer: Aether uses an optimized policy evaluation engine with pre-compiled policies, attribute caching, and smart indexing. Our architecture supports horizontal scaling with policy distribution across multiple nodes, delivering sub-millisecond latency at millions of requests per second.

diffPublic.title: What is the difference between private cloud and public cloud?
diffPublic.answer: Private cloud provides dedicated infrastructure for a single organization, offering enhanced security, customization, and performance. Public cloud shares resources across multiple tenants.

dataCenter.title: Can I choose my data center location?
dataCenter.answer: Yes, we offer multiple data center locations across Europe, North America, and Asia. You can select the region that best meets your compliance and latency requirements.

migration.title: What migration support do you offer?
migration.answer: Our team provides comprehensive migration assistance including planning, execution, and validation. We ensure minimal downtime during the transition.

billing.title: How does billing work?
billing.answer: We offer flexible billing options including monthly and annual plans. Pricing is based on your selected configuration with no hidden fees.

commitment.title: Is there a minimum commitment?
commitment.answer: We offer both short-term and long-term contracts to match your needs. Contact us to discuss the best option for your organization.

hero.title: Secure Identity for Autonomous AI Agents

capabilities.title: Core Capabilities

capabilities.aiNativeDesc: Purpose-built identity layer for AI agents with credential management, session handling, and permission scoping designed for autonomous systems.
capabilities.credentialsDesc: Secure credential issuance for AI agents with automatic rotation, scoped permissions, and usage tracking for every agent identity.
capabilities.meshDesc: Enable secure inter-agent communication with verified identities, capability-based access, and audit trails across your agent fleet.
capabilities.securityDesc: Risk-based policies that adapt to agent behavior with anomaly detection, rate limiting, and automatic threat response.
capabilities.workflowsDesc: Define identity-based workflows that agents can execute with explicit permissions and multi-step approval chains.
capabilities.observabilityDesc: Complete audit logs, metrics, and traces for every agent action with real-time dashboards and alerting.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Common questions about Aether for AI Agents.

diffIam.title: How is Aether for AI Agents different from regular IAM?
diffIam.answer: Aether for AI Agents is purpose-built for autonomous AI systems. It handles agent-specific concepts like capability scoping, inter-agent communication, workflow execution, and adaptive security policies that traditional IAM systems don't support.

llm.title: Can I use Aether with my existing LLM provider?
llm.answer: Yes, Aether integrates with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and any LangChain-compatible model. We're continuously adding new integrations.

credentialSecurity.title: How do you handle agent credential security?
credentialSecurity.answer: We use hardware security modules (HSM) for key storage, automatic credential rotation, short-lived tokens, and comprehensive audit logging. Credentials never leave your secure infrastructure.

freeTier.title: Is there a free tier for testing?
freeTier.answer: Yes, Aether Community Edition is free and includes up to 100 agents. For larger deployments, contact our sales team.
